SEARCH
Package com.example.junimoapp

Class AdminUnitTest

java.lang.Object
com.example.junimoapp.AdminUnitTest
public class AdminUnitTest extends Object
Unit tests for admin functionality. These tests cover the model and logic used by the admin browse/delete features.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    AdminUnitTest()
     

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    testCommentItemCreation()
    US 03.10.01 As an admin, I want to remove comments Tests CommentItem stores correct stuff
    void
    testDeletingNonExistentEventDoesNotCrash()
    US 03.01.01 Tests that trying to delete an event that doesn't exist in the list does not crash and leaves the list unchanged.
    void
    testEventItemCreation()
    US 03.04.01 - As an administrator, I want to be able to browse events.
    void
    testEventItemHandlesEmptyFields()
    US 03.04.01 Tests that when an event has an empty string for title, the placeholder logic still correctly substitutes the fallback.
    void
    testEventItemHandlesMissingFields()
    US 03.04.01 Tests that when an event has no title or description (missing Firestore fields), our placeholder logic correctly substitutes readable fallback strings.
    void
    testEventItemHasValidDocumentIdForDeletion()
    US 03.01.01 - As an administrator, I want to be able to remove events.
    void
    testEventRemovedFromListAfterDeletion()
    US 03.01.01 Tests that after simulating a deletion from a list, the event is actually removed and the list size decreases by 1.
    void
    testImageItemCreation()
    US 03.06.01 As an admin, I want to browse images Tests ImageItem created correctly (simulates loading and wrapping image from Firestore)
    void
    testImageRemovedFromListAfterDeletion()
    US 03.03.01 As an admin, I want to remove images Tests item is gone after simulated removal
    void
    testNotificationItemCreation()
    US 03.08.01 As an admin, I want to review notification logs Tests NotifItem correctly created, simulating reading/wrapping notification
    void
    testOrganizerItemCreationForDemotion()
    US 03.05.01 As an administrator, I want to remove organizers that violate app policy Simulates organizer creation
    void
    testUserItemCreation()
    US 03.02.01 As an administrator, I want to be able to browse profiles Testing that the UserItem is made correctly with the expected fields
    void
    testUserItemHandlesMissingFields()
    US 03.02.01 Tests missing Firestore fields (placeholder fallbacks)
    void
    testUserItemHasValidDocumentIdForDeletion()
    US 03.03.01 As an administratos, I want to be able to remove profiles Tests that a UserItem intended for deletion has a documentID since that's what's passed to the Firestore's delete call.
    void
    testUserRemovedFromListAfterDeletion()
    US 3.03.01 Tests that after a deletion from the list, the user is actually removed and the list size is decreased by 1

  • Constructor Details

    • AdminUnitTest

      public AdminUnitTest()

  • Method Details

    • testEventItemCreation

      public void testEventItemCreation()
      US 03.04.01 - As an administrator, I want to be able to browse events. Tests that an EventItem is created correctly with the expected field values. This simulates what happens when we load an event from Firestore and wrap it in an EventItem for the admin to browse.

    • testEventItemHandlesMissingFields

      public void testEventItemHandlesMissingFields()
      US 03.04.01 Tests that when an event has no title or description (missing Firestore fields), our placeholder logic correctly substitutes readable fallback strings. This mirrors the null-handling code in AdminBrowseEventsActivity.loadEvents().

    • testEventItemHandlesEmptyFields

      public void testEventItemHandlesEmptyFields()
      US 03.04.01 Tests that when an event has an empty string for title, the placeholder logic still correctly substitutes the fallback.

    • testEventItemHasValidDocumentIdForDeletion

      public void testEventItemHasValidDocumentIdForDeletion()
      US 03.01.01 - As an administrator, I want to be able to remove events. Tests that an EventItem intended for deletion has a valid non-null documentId. The documentId is what gets passed to Firestore's .delete() call, so if it's null or empty, deletion would fail silently or crash.

    • testEventRemovedFromListAfterDeletion

      public void testEventRemovedFromListAfterDeletion()
      US 03.01.01 Tests that after simulating a deletion from a list, the event is actually removed and the list size decreases by 1. This mirrors the logic in AdminBrowseEventsActivity.deleteEventFromFirestore() where we remove the item from the local list after Firestore confirms deletion.

    • testDeletingNonExistentEventDoesNotCrash

      public void testDeletingNonExistentEventDoesNotCrash()
      US 03.01.01 Tests that trying to delete an event that doesn't exist in the list does not crash and leaves the list unchanged. Covers the (position != -1) guard in deleteEventFromFirestore().

    • testUserItemCreation

      public void testUserItemCreation()
      US 03.02.01 As an administrator, I want to be able to browse profiles Testing that the UserItem is made correctly with the expected fields

    • testUserItemHandlesMissingFields

      public void testUserItemHandlesMissingFields()
      US 03.02.01 Tests missing Firestore fields (placeholder fallbacks)

    • testUserItemHasValidDocumentIdForDeletion

      public void testUserItemHasValidDocumentIdForDeletion()
      US 03.03.01 As an administratos, I want to be able to remove profiles Tests that a UserItem intended for deletion has a documentID since that's what's passed to the Firestore's delete call. If it's null or empty, it will fail

    • testUserRemovedFromListAfterDeletion

      public void testUserRemovedFromListAfterDeletion()
      US 3.03.01 Tests that after a deletion from the list, the user is actually removed and the list size is decreased by 1

    • testOrganizerItemCreationForDemotion

      public void testOrganizerItemCreationForDemotion()
      US 03.05.01 As an administrator, I want to remove organizers that violate app policy Simulates organizer creation

    • testImageItemCreation

      public void testImageItemCreation()
      US 03.06.01 As an admin, I want to browse images Tests ImageItem created correctly (simulates loading and wrapping image from Firestore)

    • testImageRemovedFromListAfterDeletion

      public void testImageRemovedFromListAfterDeletion()
      US 03.03.01 As an admin, I want to remove images Tests item is gone after simulated removal

    • testNotificationItemCreation

      public void testNotificationItemCreation()
      US 03.08.01 As an admin, I want to review notification logs Tests NotifItem correctly created, simulating reading/wrapping notification

    • testCommentItemCreation

      public void testCommentItemCreation()
      US 03.10.01 As an admin, I want to remove comments Tests CommentItem stores correct stuff
